data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Simplifies user choice by limiting available options.
data.technical_spec: Google Search interface designed with minimal distractions to enhance focus.

data.title: Fitts’ Law
data.description: Optimizes target size and distance for frequently used features.
data.technical_spec: Key features like the search bar are prominently sized and centrally located for easy access.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limits cognitive load by grouping information into digestible chunks.
data.technical_spec: Use of well-structured categories and clean design to avoid overwhelming users.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Designs interfaces that are consistent with user expectations built from other websites.
data.technical_spec: Google retains a consistent design language recognizable across its services.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Enhances user perception of usability through visually appealing design.
data.technical_spec: Clean, minimalistic interface that enhances user interaction experience.
